title: About
text: My name is Noora Mohamed, I am a junior at the University of Nebraska, Lincoln. I double major in Sports media and Journalism, with a minor in Communication studies and Textile, Merchandising fashion design. I recently started my masters in Integrated media Communication at UNL.

title: HOW DOES CHANGING IN COACHES AFFECT A RANKING OF A TEAM
text:
The results of a game, always comes back to what the coaches teach behind closed doors. Coaches are expected to train, discipline and prepare their athletes for every possibility in the field.
Fans focus on the winning and scoring, the real impact all comes back to the objective, planning and knowledge, that coaches implant in their players.
The scores of a single game, leads back to what the coach taught and how successfully the players executed that vision, revealing the ability the coach has when it comes to playing offense.
Álvaro Arbeloa-
Álvaro Arbeloa, a Spanish former defender football player professional footballer and current manager. Arbeloa has won many trophies; Club World Cup, a UEFA Super Cup, a LaLiga Title, two Copas del Rey and a Spanish Super Cup. Shifting from a player into a coach, alongside his passion and management that highlighting his career.
Xabi Alonso-
Xabi Alonso a former Midfielder football player and a former coach in Real Madrid. Alonso has apperated in about 236 competitive games, wining over 9 trophis during his time as a player, his trophies included 1 LaLiga title and 2 European Championships with Spain. Alonso is considered as one of the best midfilers of his generations.

Carlo Ancelotti-
Carlo Ancellotti, the former coach of Real Madrid and the most decorated coach in the history of Real Madrid, wining over 15 trophies. Ancellotti has started his career in July 2009, coaching AC Milan for eight years.
How much does a change of coach play in team ranking?


Code
library(tidyverse)
madrid <- read_csv("LALIGA25-26.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) |> filter(Pts >0)|> mutate(season="25-26")
madrid1 <- read_csv("LALIGA24-25.csv",show_col_types = FALSE)|> filter(Pts>0)|>
mutate(season="24-25")
madrid2<- read_csv("LALIGA23-24.csv",show_col_types = FALSE)|> filter(Pts>0)|>
mutate(season="23-24")
madrid_all<-bind_rows(madrid,madrid1,madrid2)
ggplot(madrid_all,aes(x=reorder(Name, RK), weight=Pts, fill=season))+
 geom_bar(position="dodge")+
             coord_flip()+
      labs(title = "How the changing of coaches has an effect on ranking",
       subtitle = "Nacho ranking was highest overall between season 23-26",
       x = "Change in Ranking number ",
       y = "Name of Team Players",
       caption = "Source: ESPN|By Noora Mohamed")+
  theme_minimal()+
theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")) 









For my hypothesis, I used ESPN to analyze and understand how coaching change, has an impact on the team’s skills and ranking. After, studying the bar charts, I discovered that a team player’s performance and ranking, demonstrates the skills and proficiency of the coach. For instance, Dean Huijsen is a Defender for Real Madrid during the LaLiga season he was number one ranked, when he was coached by Alvaro Arbeloa, who was also a former defender player. The bar chart results, demonstrates that when a coach changes it can in fact have an impact on team performances when coaches apply their experience into the field.


Code
madrid25 <- read_csv("25RFBF.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
madrid24<- read_csv("24FBREF.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
madrid23<- read_csv("23-24fb.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
ggplot() + 
  geom_line(data=madrid25, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="red") + 
  geom_line(data=madrid24, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="darkgrey") +
  geom_line(data=madrid23, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="blue") + 
  labs(title = "THE GOALS SCORED FOR THE LALIGA SEASON FOR REAL MADRID VS THE GOALS SCORED AGAINST FROM 23-26 ",
       subtitle = "REAL MADRID OFFESNVIE AND DEFESNEIVE TELLS A DIFFERENT STORY ACROSS THE THREE SEASON OF 2023-2024,2024-2025 AND 2025-2026 ",
       x = "GOALS FOR",
       y = "GAMES AGAINST",
       caption = "Source: ESPN|By: Noora Mohamed")









Code
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 10))


<ScaleContinuousPosition>
 Range:  
 Limits:    0 --   10


When trying to understand the various ways Real Madrid played during the LaLiga season, it’s important to look at goals such as; Goals For (GF) and Goals against (GA) utilizing these two different data, gives a chance to understand the strength the team has when tackling with goals and also help’s to evaluate the team’s perfomance and strength when it comes to scoring and the tatics that lead to this point.
I used Football Ref to get better insights about the goals across the season, creating a line chart has helped me evaluate the team strength better and understand what season did they do better in goal’s.
Studying a chart about the Goals, gives more information about the teams defesne, pace and tatics when it comes to balls.


Code
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork) 
gamess <- read_csv("25_26.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
gamesss<- read_csv("23_24.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
bar1 <- ggplot() + 
  geom_col(data=gamess, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Position),y=LALIGA), fill="blue") + geom_col(data=gamesss, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Position),y=LALIGA), fill="red") + coord_flip() + 
  labs( x="Points", y="Team Ranking") + 
  theme_minimal() 
bar2 <- 
  ggplot() + geom_col(data=gamesss, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Wins),y=LALIGA), fill="darkgrey") + 
  geom_col(data=gamess, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Wins),y=LALIGA), fill="pink") + coord_flip() +
  labs( x="Points", y="Team Ranking") + 
  theme_minimal() 
bar1/bar2 + 
  plot_annotation( title = "Real Madrid LALIGA ranking", 
  subtitle = "During the LALIGA 2023 season Real Madrid was ranked number one by 95 points. Following the 2026 season, Real Madrid is now ranked second, with Barcelona taking the lead by 76 points.",
  caption = "Source: Yahoo Sports | By Noora Mohamed" ) & theme ( 
  plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), 
  axis.title = element_text(size = 8), plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), panel.grid.minor = element_blank() )









To better understand the difference of ranking, it was not only about the team for me but, rather the difference of players. I compared Real Madrid and Barcelona by comparing two differences, of results during the LaLiga season in the year 2025-2026 and using 2023-2024 to see the difference, giving us the chance to look at the changes overtime.
Looking at team as a whole speaks a story about not only the coaching but, what has happened during the game and the season that lead to Real Madrid ranking going down. For instance, Real Madrid was first ranked during the year 2023 to 2024 with high points of
